logs_chat_count_description: The total number of new conversations created in the app. A new conversation is created if the interval between the new message and the last message exceeds 15 minutes. (This definition only applies here.)

logs_error_rate_description: The proportion of chats that encountered error to total chats

show_top_p_tip: Nucleus sampling is an alternative to temperature sampling. By using this method, the model considers only tokens whose cumulative probability exceeds p. For example, if p is set to 0.1, the model will consider only tokens with the highest cumulative probability. Default value: 1.
simple_tool_tips: The tool cannot be called by simple apps because it contains special inputs.
stop_sign_placeholder: Separate multiple sequences with the pipe character (|). Example: aaa|stop
stream_response_tip: If disabled, the model will not output contents immediately, but wait until the entire response is generated. Therefore, it can obtain the output contents for further processing.

temperature_tip: Range: 0-10 A higher value leads to more creative outputs, while a lower value leads to more deterministic outputs.

variable.select type_desc:
Configure global variables for a workflow. These global variables are typically used for temporary caching. Variables can be specified in the following ways:
1. Use query parameters on the chat page.
2. Use variables in the API request.
3. Use a node with the variable update tool.

smart_customer_service_no_knowledge_foun: Method when no knowledge is found

retrieval_mode_tooltip:
Standard search: Find relevant knowledge through a single search, fast response, suitable for simple Q&A scenarios.
Multi-round intelligent search: Intelligent analysis and decomposition of problems, through multiple rounds of search to gradually supplement and filter knowledge, suitable for complex problems (longer response time).
retrieval_mode_single_desc: Find relevant knowledge through a single search, fast response, suitable for simple Q&A scenarios.
retrieval_mode_multiple_desc: Intelligent analysis and decomposition of problems, through multiple rounds of search to gradually supplement and filter knowledge, suitable for complex problems (longer response time).
retrieval_output_thinking_tooltip: Enable the streaming output of the thinking process of the intelligent search in the chat, including the decomposed questions and the search results of each round.
retrieval_multiple_ai_model_tip: Used for analyzing and decomposing problems, filtering, and determining whether the search content is sufficient to answer the question.
smart_customer_service_ai_model_tip_agen: Used for analyzing and decomposing problems, filtering, and determining whether the search content is sufficient to answer the question.
smart_customer_service_ai_model_tip_stan: Used for rewriting questions, generating SQL statements (if applicable), etc.

agent_desc: Associate the required tools and skills to enable AI to call and plan autonomously.

Agent mode is in the testing phase and may undergo significant changes in interaction/scheduling logic in the future. It is recommended to use it only online and not to connect it to your production system.
The credit consumed by agent mode is tens of times that of ordinary apps. Please use it with caution.

space_to_expand_folder: Expand folder by pressing the space bar.
workflow_requirement_default:
Service background:
Trigger conditions:
Core process:
Input:
Output requirements:
workflow_requirement_tooltip_example:
[Service Background]
We are a SaaS enterprise service company, and customers submit service tickets via WeCom, email, and website forms.
Current pain points: Large number of tickets, low efficiency of manual classification and assignment, and slow response to urgent issues.
[Trigger Conditions]
Workflow starts when a customer message is received via any of the following channels:
- WeCom customer service group @ robot
- New email received in the after-sales mailbox
- Submit a form on the official website's Technical Support page.
[Core Process]
1. Receive work orders: Extract customer information (company name, contact person, priority tag)
2. Intent recognition: Determine the work order type (technical issue, account problem, feature inquiry, complaint/suggestion, business cooperation).
3. Severity assessment: Keywords ("outage"/"unable to log in"/"urgent"/"paying customer") + customer level
4. Smart processing:
- Technical Issue → Search Knowledge Base → If Match Score > 0.8, automatically reply with solution.
- Account issue → Call internal CRM to query account status → Generate diagnosis report
- Feature inquiry → Search product manual → Generate reply with reference source
- Complaints and suggestions → Record in Feishu multi-dimensional table → Notify the customer success manager
- Business cooperation → Transfer to sales department → Automatically send confirmation email to the customer.
5. Manual fallback: If the knowledge base does not hit or the customer feedbacks "Not resolved" → Create a Jira work order and assign it to the on-duty engineer.
6. Closed-loop feedback: After resolution, automatically send satisfaction survey and write the results to the CRM.
[Input]
- User input: work order title, detailed description, and attachments (screenshots/logs)
- System data: Customer level (free, standard, enterprise, flagship), number of historical work orders, SLA agreement
- Knowledge base: product FAQ, technical troubleshooting guide, API documentation
[Output Requirements]
- Reply format: WeCom Markdown card/email HTML
- Tone: Professional and concise. Technical issues should include troubleshooting steps.
- Must include: Knowledge base reference source (document name + paragraph), and commitment to processing time (e.g., "Engineer will respond within 2 hours")
[Exception Handling]
- When the customer sends 3 messages consecutively without resolution: Automatically escalate to the supervisor group and @ the on-duty manager.
- When the knowledge base retrieval confidence is < 0.6: Do not auto-reply and directly transfer to a human.
- When an API call times out (> 5 seconds): Return a friendly message "Querying... Please wait." and retry asynchronously.
publish_channel.wecom.empty: Before publishing to WeCom robot, please bind a custom domain and pass domain name verification.
rerank_model_training_warning: This model will be used for training. It cannot be modified after the app is created.
